themes.summary: TSMC detailed its technology roadmap, including 6T SRAM breakthroughs, 98% yield for CoWoS 5.5x reticle size, and aggressive N2/A16 capacity expansion (70% CAGR 2026-2028). AI and HPC chips are projected to account for 55% of the semiconductor market by 2030, with TSMC manufacturing Qualcomm's new data center CPUs and OpenAI's custom AI inference chips.

themes.summary: The memory market is in a structural upcycle with rising DRAM and HBM prices, expected to persist beyond 2030 due to AI-driven demand. Micron's FYQ3 EPS guidance aligns with expectations, with traditional DRAM prices rising ~45% and HBM prices projected to be 80% higher by end-CY2027.

InP fabs and 6-inch capacity are critical for this expansion.

themes.summary: NVIDIA's NVFP4 quantization significantly reduces the HBM footprint for AI models, cutting GLM-5.2 model-weight memory by ~69% compared to non-quantized versions. This efficiency allows running large models on fewer Blackwell-class GPUs, reducing serving costs without degrading output quality.

themes.summary: The upstream PCB supply chain is experiencing a supercycle with critical friction concentrated in glass fabric, copper foil, and Copper Clad Laminate. Supply deficits for glass fabric are noted as substantially more severe than for copper foil, alongside relentless capacity constraints in the IC substrate sector.

mentions.text: People don't quite understand how many behind the meter power generation assets are being built for datacenters because the US Grid sucks, despite the higher cost and complexity.

themes.topic: Qualcomm's High Bandwidth Compute for AI
themes.summary: Qualcomm plans to integrate its High Bandwidth Compute architecture, which vertically stacks memory and compute, into smartphones, PCs, and cars. This aims to improve data flow and enable more local AI models and always-on agents on mobile devices.

mentions.text:
Qualcomm $QCOM plans to bring parts of its new data-center chip architecture to smartphones, PCs and cars, per Semafor.

Its High Bandwidth Compute architecture stacks memory & compute vertically to improve data flow, potentially enabling more local AI models and always-on agents

themes.summary: Morgan Stanley maintains its forecast for the Federal Reserve to hold interest rates through year-end, with incoming data since the June FOMC meeting making them "marginally more comfortable" with a no-hike baseline.

mentions.text:
Morgan Stanley: Retained its forecast for the Federal Reserve to hold interest rates through year-end (no rate hikes). 

Michael Gapen said incoming data since the June FOMC meeting has made it "marginally more comfortable" in its no-hike baseline. https://t.co/9OL7od94JP

themes.summary: The economic value of AI models automating jobs is complex; while the marginal contribution of an additional AI instance might be low due to competition, the total contribution to GDP will be significantly higher due to widespread adoption and parallel operation.

mentions.text:
Suppose an AI model fully automates a $200k job. Its economic value - measured in contribution to GDP - will be both far more and far less than $200k.

There are two ways of thinking about the model's economic value:
